# Home {.unnumbered .hide-heading}

This website is for resources and materials related to HHCR 710 and 711

::: {.column-margin}
![Learning statistics may require pushing yourself to try things you have previously chosen to avoid.](img/lkh_flying.png){#fig-leap fig-align="right" .lightbox}
:::

## Resources for specific classes

### Biostatistics Foundations I

The examples and problems for this chapter can be loaded into Stata by running the following command:

```stata
run https://tphofer.github.io/ncsp-hhcr/code/biostat1.do
```

(nothing will happen when you run this command but it will load the examples and problems into your stata environment so that you can run the commands in the examples and problems below)

### Biostatistics Foundations II

```stata
run https://tphofer.github.io/ncsp-hhcr/code/biostat2.do
```

## Course resources

The textbook is [located here](https://websites.umich.edu/~thofer/teach/hhcr-book/).

### hypothes.is

This is a "social annotation web-based tool" recommended by the University called (somewhat un-intuitively) "Hypothes.is" that I am trying out.

1. For sharing knowledge between yourselves before class to help understand the matieral.
2. To allow me to identify sections that need to be changed. (Identification of omissions, mistakes and typos are also welcome)

You can set up the tool here:  https://web.hypothes.is/web-app-start/
by setting up an account and installing the extension or bookmarklet depending on your default browser.

Then you can join the group for the textbook by clicking on this link:  https://hypothes.is/groups/p2deDxvP/ncsp-textbook

Once installed you can go to the website of the textbook ( https://websites.umich.edu/~thofer/teach/hhcr-book/ ) , click the bookmarklet or turn on the extension and you will be able to select the NCSP-textbook group and annotate , ask questions and reply to other students questions.  All the comments or questions can be seen by everyone in the group and provide a way to discuss the material before class.

### iclicker 

```html
https://join.iclicker.com/LVQX
```

(when you are asked to create an account just make sure to use your <uniqname@umich.edu> address **not** your med.umich.edu address)
